nodes.title: Scientific Basis of Human Nutr
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Medical Nutrition Therapy I
nodes.description: The first of a two-course series exploring concepts in pathophysiology of disease, with a focus on nutrition interventions. Explores foundational aspects of nutritional assessment and the overall nutritional care process. Prerequisites: NFS 2143, Senior standing, NFS Dietetics Concentration major.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing, NFS Dietetics Concentration major

nodes.title: Evidence-based Practice Prjct
nodes.description: On site identification, review of literature for background and possible solutions, data collection and analysis, and writing and presenting the results and conclusions of a research problem. Pre/co-requisites: Successful completion of the first year of the Master of Science in Dietetics program.
nodes.other_requirements: Successful completion of the first year of the Master of Science in Dietetics program. MSD graduate students only

nodes.title: Ugr Hum Anatomy & Physiology 2
nodes.description: Part II of two-semester course sequence. Structure and function of human body. Prerequisite: ANPS 1190.
nodes.other_requirements: Common hour exams are held 5:30-7:00pm on these Mondays: 2/9, 3/2, and 4/6. BIOM,CSD,DIET,DNFS,EXSC,HSCI,MLS,NFS, PE, PHS, MRS, PRNU; Majors only until Dec. 2nd, Please contact Patrick Mullen (patrick.c.mullen@uvm.edu) with any questions; Class meets 3x a week with online lab comprising 4th credit; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE students register for ANPS 1200 ZRA; Prereq enforced by the system:ANPS 1190.

nodes.title: Master's Project Research
nodes.description: Final project under the direction of a graduate faculty mentor. Prerequisite: Nutrition & Food Sciences non-thesis student; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Nutrition & Food Sciences non-thesis student; Instructor permission. Instructor permission required

nodes.title: U.S. Food Policy and Politics
nodes.description: Provides a systems perspective on U.S. food policies and politics across the food system. Focuses on understanding the U.S. food policy process, policymakers, stakeholders, issues, goals and feedbacks between food policy and politics. Prerequisites: NFS 1073 or CDAE 1020 or CDAE 1040. Cross-listed with: FS 2010.

nodes.title: Survey of the Field
nodes.description: Nutrition and Food Sciences introduction to the professional field and career opportunities in dietetics, nutrition and food science. Required of all First-Year and transfer students. Fall. Prerequisite: Nutrition and Food Science majors and Dietetics, Nutrition and Food Science majors only, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Nutrition and Food Science majors and Dietetics, Nutrition and Food Science majors only, or Instructor permission. NFS majors only, or instructor permission

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Intro to Research Methods
nodes.description: Basic introduction to research methods at the Master's level, including formulation of a research question and hypothesis, literature searching and preparation of a literature review, analytical methods and experimental design, data analysis and presentation, and journal article publication.

nodes.title: Clinical Nutrition 2
nodes.description: The second of a two-course series exploring concepts in pathophysiology of disease, with a focus on nutrition interventions. Builds on foundational aspects of nutritional assessment and the overall nutrition care process. Major organ system dysfunction including cardiovascular, renal, endocrine, pulmonary, central nervous system and immune processes/metabolic stress will be discussed. Prerequisites: NFS 4260, Nutrition and Food Science major with Dietetics concentration, Senior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Nutrition and Food Science major with Dietetics concentration, Senior standing. PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Introduction to Biochemistry
nodes.description: Exploring biological processes at the molecular level and how they are controlled. Topics include enzymes, gene expression, and metabolism of carbohydrates and lipids. Restricted to Nutrition and Food Sciences and Dietetics, Nutrition and Food Sciences majors; others by Instructor permission. Prerequisites: CHEM 1580; or CHEM 2580 and CHEM 2585; or other acceptable coursework in organic chemistry.
nodes.other_requirements: Restricted to Nutrition and Food Sciences and Dietetics, Nutrition and Food Sciences majors; others by Instructor permission. acceptable coursework in organic chemistry. NFS majors only

nodes.title: MSD Journal Club
nodes.description: Critical review of current scientific, peer-reviewed literature, student-led facilitated discussions, abstract writing on topics related to nutrition, sustainable food systems, hunger and food insecurity, health promotion, chronic disease prevention and management. Prerequisite: Master of Science in Dietetics student.
nodes.other_requirements: Master of Science in Dietetics student. MSD students only

nodes.title: US Food, Social Equity &Dev
nodes.description: Provides an introduction to gender, race, class, and ethnicity with particular emphasis on food, population, economic, and ecological issues in sustainable agriculture, food systems, and community development. The geographical focus emphasizes the United States.

nodes.title: Microbiol & Infectious Disease
nodes.description: An introduction to basic microbiology and microbes that cause infectious diseases, with a focus on microbial structure, function, metabolism, ecology, and pathogenesis. Prerequisites: BHSC 1340, BCOR 1400, BCOR 1425, BIOL 1005 or BIOL 1400; Microbiology or Molecular Genetics major or minor or Animal Sciences, Biomedical and Clinical Sciences major.
nodes.other_requirements: Microbiology or Molecular Genetics major or minor or Animal Sciences, Biomedical and Clinical Sciences major. Minimum sophomore standing; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE and Post Bac Pre Med students register for MMG 2010 ZRA; Eligible majors: ASCI, BISC, ENSC, MGEN, MICR, MLS, PBIO, HSCI, PHS,BMCS majors and MICR minors only until 5/01/2026.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Ugr Hum Anatomy & Physiology 1
nodes.description: Part I of two-semester course sequence. Structure and function of human body.
nodes.other_requirements: Prereg limited to: BMCS, BIOM, CSD, EMS, EXSC, HSCI, MLS, MRS, NFS, NMT, PE, PHS, PRNU majors only. Degree students only even after major restrictions lifted.

nodes.title: Supervised Practice I
nodes.description: Through lecture, discussion, presentations, and practical experience, students develop competencies in clinical dietetics, community nutrition, and food service management. Prerequisite: Master of Science in Dietetics student.
nodes.other_requirements: Master of Science in Dietetics student. MSD students only

nodes.title: Foodservice Systems
nodes.description: Emphasis on the foodservice system model for understanding quality control; food procurement, production, and marketing; management and evaluation of foodservice facilities, human and financial resources. Prerequisites: NFS 1043, NFS 1053, NFS 2143, minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing; PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Nutrition
nodes.description: The study of standard guidelines to select foods that maximize human health and the functions of the essential nutrients needed to sustain human life. Prerequisites: High school chemistry and biology.
nodes.other_requirements: High school chemistry and biology. HS or College CHEM or BIO;

nodes.title: Food Chemistry: Macronutrients
nodes.description: An examination of the properties and functions of macromolecules in food, including water, proteins, carbohydrates, and lipids. Topics include protein structure, starch structure, gelatinization, lipid oxidation, and the role they play in food texture, stability, and nutritional quality. Prerequisite: CHEM 1580.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE Student by Permission and Override

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Department permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Department permission. Instructor permission required

nodes.title: HACCP: Theory & Application
nodes.description: This course addresses the development of a HACCP plan. Requirements of both the USDA-FSIS and FDA are examined. A mock HACCP plan will be developed. Prerequisites: NFS 3203 and Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Intro Organic Chemistry w/lab
nodes.description: Properties and reactivity of basic organic compounds of technological and biological significance. Not recommended for pre-medical students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1580 and CHEM 1150, CHEM 1152, CHEM 1582, CHEM 1500, or CHEM 2580. Prerequisite: CHEM 1100 or CHEM 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: Not recommended for pre-medical students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1580 and  CHEM 1150 ,  CHEM 1152 ,  CHEM 1582 ,  CHEM 1500 , or  CHEM 2580 . Must register for a CHEM 1580 lab, L01-L15; $85 fee: Chem Lab Supplies; Cross listed withCHEM 1582A. PACE students with permission and override. All labs must be completed in order to pass the course. Attendance for all lectures and exams is expected. All work must be completed by the due date. The final exam must be taken to pass the course.

nodes.title: Human Health in the Food Syst
nodes.description: Explores the multifaceted and evolving intersection of food systems, dietary quality, food availability and human health outcomes. Investigates how political, economic, social and cultural drivers in the food system influence human health outcomes. Prerequisites: NFS 1043 or NFS 1073. Cross-listed with: FS 2030.

nodes.title: Functional Foods:Prncpl & Tech
nodes.description: Examines the constituents that make food products functional and provides laboratory techniques needed to create a functional food. Prerequisites: NFS 2153, NFS 2154, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Food Science Concentration Seniors only or instructor permission; PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Nutrition for Global Health
nodes.description: An upper-level course designed to expose students to contemporary issues in public health nutrition in a global setting, with an emphasis on maternal and child nutrition in low and middle-income countries. The course has a particular focus on the interplay between demographic, nutritional and epidemiologic transitions. Use of case studies from different countries and world regions to explore public health nutrition issues of importance, and efforts to control and prevent hunger, malnutrition, and diet-related problems Prerequisites: NFS 1043, NFS 2143, NFS 3243; Senior Nutrition and Food Sciences majors and minors only.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior Nutrition and Food Sciences majors and minors only. Senior standing only; NFS majors and minors only.

nodes.title: Nutrition in the Life Cycle
nodes.description: Nutritional needs of people throughout the life cycle. Physiological and environmental factors which affect nutritional status. Designed for Nutrition majors. Prerequisite: NFS 1043.

nodes.title: Kitchen Science
nodes.description: Integrated lecture-lab course that explores the scientific concepts underlying why foods do what they do in the kitchen. Applications include topics such as ice cream, gluten, and molecular gastronomy. Labs and final project provide opportunities to design, conduct, and evaluate experiments investigating culinary phenomena. Prerequisite: Nutrition and Food Sciences major or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Nutrition and Food Sciences major or Instructor permission. Non-NFS majors should email Professor Almstead (Laura.Almstead@uvm.edu) to be put on a waiting list for the course.

nodes.title: Community Practicum
nodes.description: Professional field experience in a community nutrition organization. Credit negotiable but not to exceed three per semester. Enrollment may be more than once, maximum of six credits. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. NFS Dietetics Concentration majors only, or instructor permission, PACE Student by Permission and Override

nodes.title: Farm to Table: Food Sys
nodes.description: This course provides an introduction to the contemporary food system, focusing on the interdependence of all components, from farm to table.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum sophomore standing, or instructor permission

nodes.title: Food Regulation
nodes.description: Comprehensive examination of US food laws and regulations and their relationships to the safety of the US food supply. Focus on how food-related laws and regulations are enacted and enforced, through detailed examination of selected food regulation topics. Prerequisite: NFS 2153 or equivalent course/training with Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Nutrition Educ & Counseling
nodes.description: Use of appropriate education theory, techniques, and media in nutrition education and counseling theories and negotiation, interviewing and counseling skills in individual and group counseling. Pre/Co-requisites: NFS 2143; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing

nodes.title: Organic Chemistry 1
nodes.description: Properties and reactivity of organic compounds with consideration of bonding, stereochemistry, and reaction mechanisms. For premedical and biological sciences students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 2580 and CHEM 1580, CHEM 1582, or CHEM 1500. Prerequisite: CHEM 1450 or CHEM 1455.
nodes.other_requirements: $85 fee for lab supplies; Must register for a CHEM 2580 lab L01-L23; Degree students only; Post Bac Pre Med students register for CHEM 2580 ZRA-ZRC

nodes.title: NFS Senior Seminar
nodes.description: Designed to help students through the process of identifying what they'd like to do with their degree after graduating from UVM, as well as prepare students to complete the required materials for future opportunities. Prerequisites: Nutrition and Food Sciences major; Senior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Nutrition and Food Sciences major; Senior standing. Senior standing NFS Dietetics Concentration students only

nodes.title: World Food,Pop & Develop
nodes.description: Agricultural development emphasizing natural and economic phenomena and the effect of food supplies on population trends and policies.

nodes.title: Principles Food Technology Lab
nodes.description: Experiential learning of principles of major modern food processing and preservation technologies, essential skills of food quality and safety assurance, and new product development. Pre/Co-requisite: NFS 2153.
nodes.other_requirements:
NFS majors and minors only
Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Basic Concepts of Foods
nodes.description: Introduces the basic concepts of food central to the disciplines of nutrition, food science and food systems. Introduces these basic concepts in the same way as everyday Americans - through the process of meal preparation.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Department permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Department permission. Instructor permission required. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Food Analysis
nodes.description: Covers basic analytical techniques used in food analysis. Students will learn physical tests (appearance, texture, specific gravity, refractive index, viscosity, rheology) and chemical analysis (moisture, ash, protein, lipid, carbohydrates, fiber, sugars, minerals, vitamins). Basic instrumental techniques will also be introduced, such as GC, HPLC, Fluorescence and FTIR. Students will learn lab safety protocols, apply appropriate methods, perform analysis and interpret results to prepare reports. Prerequisite: CHEM 1580. Co-requisite: NFS 2210 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Co-requisite: NFS 2210 or Instructor permission. PACE Students by Permission and Override

nodes.title: Deadly Food: Outbreak Investig
nodes.description: Investigates how U.S. public health officials discover, investigate, and solve foodborne outbreaks. Introduces common pathogens and foods involved in outbreaks in the U.S., the laboratory and investigative methods officials use to solve the outbreaks, and the government agencies involved. The second half of the semester will focus on case studies. Pre/Co-requisites: NFS 1053, NFS 1072, MMG 2010, ASCI 1000; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Introductory Animal Sciences
nodes.description: An overview of the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, and management of livestock and recreation species; introduction to animal behavior, animal disease, and biotechnology. Prerequisite: Animal Science major, minor, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Animal Science major, minor, or Instructor permission. Must register for an ASCI 1000 lab; ASCI first-year students only; Materials fee: $51; One mandatory Wed seminar

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Departmental permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Departmental permission. NFS students only, or instructor permission; PACE Student by Permission and Override

nodes.title: What's Brewing in Food Science
nodes.description: This course will explore food science via the production of beer and other fermented beverages. Students will also identify mechanisms to modify their drinking habits.

nodes.title: Organic Chemistry 2
nodes.description: Reactivity of organic compounds and applications to synthesis. Spectroscopy is discussed in relation to compound characterization. For premedical and biological sciences students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 2585 and CHEM 1550. Prerequisite: CHEM 1500 or CHEM 2580.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for a CHEM 2585 lab, L01-L20; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre Med students register for CHEM 2585 ZRA; Fee: $85

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Final research thesis under the direction of a graduate faculty mentor.

nodes.title: Food Safety for Food Service
nodes.description: This course will prepare students for the ServSafe Certification Exam. The topics include food safety and proper food handling in a restaurant setting.

nodes.title: Basic Concepts of Foods Lab
nodes.description: Introductory level special topics courses.

nodes.title: Advanced Nutrition
nodes.description: Study of nutrients and their specific functions in metabolic process integrating cellular physiology, biochemistry, and nutrition. Prerequisites: ANPS 1190, ANPS 1200, and NFS 2183; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing

nodes.title: Food Microbiology
nodes.description: Foodborne pathogens and spoilage microorganisms of commercial and epidemiological relevance. Conditions favorable to microbial growth, evaluation of foods for microbial content, and measures to prevent/reduce/eliminate potential microbe related food borne illness. Positive uses of microbes in the production of foods are also discussed (i.e. fermentation). Prerequisite: NFS 2153, NFS 2156 or NFS 2183.
nodes.other_requirements: instructor permission; NFS majors only; Must register for one lab NFS 3204A-C

nodes.title: Principles of Food Technology
nodes.description: Food processing technologies and underlining principles of changes in microbiological quality and safety, chemical composition and nutritional value, and interaction of functional additives and ingredients. Prerequisite: CHEM 1580; organic chemistry.
nodes.other_requirements: NFS majors, and NFS minors only; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Food Microbiology Lab
nodes.description: Introduces microbiological techniques such as Gram Stain, Streak for Isolation, dilutions, aseptic technique as well as means of identifying the microbial content of food products. Pre/Co-requisite: NFS 3203.
nodes.other_requirements: NFS majors only; Must register for lecture NFS 3203; Lab begins 1st day; ML SCI 257 is in the Carrigan Wing; Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Sports Nutrition
nodes.description: Timing and composition of meals for training and pre- and post-competition. Prerequisite: NFS 1043 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Prereqs enforced by the system: prereq override

nodes.title: Community Nutrition
nodes.description: Study of U.S. public health nutrition policies, programs and practices. Emphasis on community nutrition program planning including needs assessment, intervention development and evaluation. Prerequisite: Minimum Junior or Graduate standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior or Graduate standing. Senior NFS majors only. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Supervised Practice II
nodes.description: Through lecture, discussion, presentations, and practical experience, students develop competencies in clinical dietetics, community nutrition, and food service management. Prerequisite: Master of Science in Dietetics student.
nodes.other_requirements: Master of Science in Dietetics student. MSD graduate students only;
